entities.name: Amasiel, Danyihayr
entities.raw_quote: the fourth part of the Oration, which is this, Amasiel, Danyihayr, &c.

entities.name: Four Angels
entities.raw_quote: four Angels which he had offended in presumtuous reading so sacred a mystery, were the daily keepers and affliers

entities.name: Oration for Eloquence
entities.raw_quote: This Holy Oration which followeth, is a certain special Oration, to obtain Eloquence

attributes.context: Elements of the Oration not to be known or spoken presumptuously

entities.name: Semet, Lamen

attributes.context: Beginning of the Oration following silence

entities.raw_quote: begin to say this Oration following seriously: Semet, Lamen, &c.

entities.name: Latin Oration/Prologue

attributes.purpose: Confession of Heart and Mouth preceding the main Oration

entities.raw_quote: Oh omnipotent and eternal God, and merciful Father, blessed before all Worlds

entities.name: King Solomon

attributes.role: Recipient of Angelic teaching, witness to the Mystery

entities.raw_quote: Solomon himself witnesseth, that a Servant of his house having found this book by chance

entities.name: Angel of the Lord

attributes.role: Delivered Book and Notes to Solomon

entities.raw_quote: an Angel brought one Book, wherein was written the Figures and Orations

entities.name: Grammar
entities.raw_quote: For Grammar hath three Notes only

entities.name: Gemeliath

attributes.context: First Grammatical Note in the Book of Solomon

entities.raw_quote: Gemeliath, that is, in the Book of the Art of God

entities.name: Dialectic/Logic
entities.raw_quote: The Dialeical Notes may be used every day... The Reason why the Dialectical Art hath two Figures onely

entities.name: Rhetoric
entities.raw_quote: The Reason why Rhetorick hath four Figures

entities.name: Apollonius
entities.raw_quote: I, Apollonius, following the power of Solomon, having disposed my self to keep his works

entities.name: Theology and Astronomy
entities.raw_quote: Theology and Astronomy... hath 7 Notes, but they are great and dangerous

summary: This chunk details the specific Orations and Notes required to acquire knowledge in the Liberal Arts, particularly Grammar, Logic, and Rhetoric. It includes warnings about the dangers of presumptuous use, instructions on lunar timing for rituals, and descriptions of the Angelic transmission of these arts to Solomon.
key_passages: four Angels which he had offended in presumtuous reading so sacred a mystery
This Holy Oration which followeth, is a certain special Oration, to obtain Eloquence
The Grammatical Art... hath three things necessary thereunto; Ordination of words and times
I, Apollonius, following the power of Solomon, having disposed my self to keep his works
Theology and Astronomy... hath 7 Notes, but they are great and dangerous
